97	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
98		mode := r.Mode
99		if mode == "" {
100			if r.Once {
101				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
102			} else {
103				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
104			}
105		}
106		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
107		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
108			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
109		}
110		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
111			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
112		}
113		return mode, idleInterval
114	}

1	package work
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5	
6		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/executeloop"
7	)
8	
9	// StopCondition is the typed work-drain terminal decision vocabulary. Keep
10	// these values stable; evidence and operator surfaces derive from them.
11	type StopCondition string
12	
13	const (
14		StopConditionDrained    StopCondition = "Drained"
15		StopConditionOnce       StopCondition = "Once"
16		StopConditionSignal     StopCondition = "Signal"
17		StopConditionBudget     StopCondition = "Budget"
18		StopConditionNoProgress StopCondition = "NoProgress"
19		StopConditionBlocked    StopCondition = "Blocked"
20	)
21	
22	// StopInput describes one possible terminal drain decision point.
23	type StopInput struct {
24		ContextErr  error
25		NoReadyWork bool
26		Once        bool
27		Mode        executeloop.Mode
28		Budget      bool
29		NoProgress  bool
30		Blocked     bool
31	}
32	
33	// StopDecision is the classified terminal decision plus the legacy-compatible
34	// loop.end exit_reason string.
35	type StopDecision struct {
36		Condition  StopCondition
37		ExitReason string
38	}
39	
40	// ClassifyStop maps scattered drain-loop terminal checks to a single typed
41	// vocabulary while preserving existing exit_reason strings.
42	func ClassifyStop(in StopInput) (StopDecision, bool) {
43		switch {
44		case in.ContextErr != nil:
45			return StopDecision{
46				Condition:  StopConditionSignal,
47				ExitReason: signalExitReason(in.ContextErr),
48			}, true
49		case in.Budget:
50			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionBudget, ExitReason: "budget"}, true
51		case in.NoProgress:
52			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionNoProgress, ExitReason: "no_progress"}, true
53		case in.Blocked:
54			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionBlocked, ExitReason: "blocked"}, true
55		case in.Once:
56			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionOnce, ExitReason: "once_complete"}, true
57		case in.NoReadyWork && in.Mode != executeloop.ModeWatch:
58			return StopDecision{Condition: StopConditionDrained, ExitReason: "drained"}, true
59		default:
60			return StopDecision{}, false
61		}
62	}
63	
64	func signalExitReason(err error) string {
65		switch err {
66		case context.Canceled:
67			return "sigint"
68		case context.DeadlineExceeded:
69			return "sigterm"
70		default:
71			return "context_cancelled"
72		}
73	}
